glossary.title: Driver terms
glossary.body: A quick glossary for this dashboard:

overview.title: What this shows
overview.body: Every TMC stepper driver your printer has, read straight from its live Klipper config — one card per motor (X, Y, each Z, the extruder…). This view is read-only: it reports what is configured and what the driver is doing right now. Tuning comes in a later step.

current.title: Run / hold current
current.body: Run current is how hard the motor is pushed while moving; hold current is the lower idle level. The live figure is what the chip actually applies — it can sit a hair off the configured value because currents come in discrete steps. A driver running hot usually means the run current is set too high for the motor.

chopper.title: StealthChop vs SpreadCycle
chopper.body: The chopper mode is how the driver shapes the coil current. SpreadCycle is precise and high-torque (good for fast X/Y) but audible; StealthChop is near-silent (good for Z and idle) but slightly softer at speed. “StealthChop < N mm/s” means it stays quiet below that speed, then switches to SpreadCycle above it.

microsteps.title: Microstepping
microsteps.body: Each full motor step is electronically subdivided into microsteps (16, 32, … 256). More microsteps = smoother, quieter motion. With interpolation on, the driver smooths up to 256 internally regardless of the configured value.

stallguard.title: StallGuard (sensorless homing)
stallguard.body: StallGuard measures motor load so the axis can home by detecting a gentle stall instead of using a physical endstop. The threshold register differs by model (sgthrs on 2209, sgt on 2130/5160, sg4_thrs on 2240). Tuning it is a later step; here we just show the configured value.

health.title: Live status
health.body: When a motor is enabled (after homing or a jog), the driver reports live flags: overtemperature, short-circuit, and open-load. A green “ok” means no faults; “warning” is an over-temperature pre-warning or open load; “fault” is a short or hard over-temperature. While the motor is disabled the driver reports nothing, so the card shows “idle”.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.body: Only some drivers (e.g. the TMC2240) have a built-in temperature sensor. On models without one — like the common TMC2209 — temperature reads “no sensor”, which is normal, not an error.

catalog.title: Where the model facts come from
catalog.body: The interface (UART / SPI), the current cap, and the supported features shown on each card come from a built-in capability map of the TMC family — verified against the Klipper / Kalico driver code. It tells you, for example, that a TMC2208 can’t do sensorless homing while a TMC2209 can, or that only the TMC2240 reports its own temperature. Electrical figures are datasheet-typical; your board, sense resistor, and cooling set the real limits.

motor.title: Assigning a motor
motor.body: Pick the stepper motor wired to each axis from a built-in catalog of 200+ motors. Klipper doesn’t know which motor you fitted — telling FilaMind unlocks its datasheet specs (holding torque, rated current, resistance, inductance), which a later step uses to recommend a safe run current and driver tuning. It’s saved on the printer and changes nothing on the driver by itself.

motorsync.title: Motor synchronization
motorsync.body: When one axis is driven by two or more motors (dual or quad-Z, dual-X), their microstep phases can drift apart and fight each other — adding noise and losing torque. The motors_sync add-on measures each motor with an accelerometer and nudges them back into phase. It’s a separate add-on; if it’s installed FilaMind can run or calibrate it here (it moves the toolhead, so it’s gated). If not installed, this just explains what it does.

monitor.title: Live monitor
monitor.body: Watch a driver in real time while it runs: temperature, StallGuard load (SG_RESULT — it falls toward zero as mechanical load rises), current scale (CS_ACTUAL), and any fault flags (overtemperature, short, open-load). The driver only reports these while the motor is enabled, so move or home the axis to see live data. Read-only — it just observes.

homing.title: How this axis homes
homing.body: Klipper finds each axis’s zero one of a few ways, and FilaMind reads which from your config rather than guessing: a physical endstop switch (the classic microswitch — most printers), sensorless homing (the TMC driver senses the motor stalling at the end of travel — no switch), or the Z probe (the same sensor used for bed mesh finds Z=0). This panel adapts to whichever your axis uses — live switch state and a plain test-home for a physical endstop, a StallGuard tuner for sensorless, or a pointer to the probe tools for a probed Z. Extra motors sharing a rail (a second Z, the extruder) don’t home on their own, so they show nothing here.

sensorless.title: Sensorless homing
sensorless.body: Some axes home without an endstop switch: the driver detects the gentle “stall” when the toolhead reaches the end of travel. The StallGuard threshold sets how sensitive that is — too low and it triggers early (stops short), too high and it never triggers (the axis grinds into the frame). This helper lets you set the threshold and test-home one axis, both behind a confirm. Adjust by feel: lower if it stops early, raise if it doesn’t stop — and keep a hand near the power the first few times. If your axis uses a physical endstop, you don’t need this.

coolstep.title: CoolStep (load-adaptive current)
coolstep.body: CoolStep lets the driver lower the coil current when the motor isn’t working hard, then raise it back under load — saving heat and noise. It’s a coupled feedback loop with five interacting registers (semin/semax/seup/sedn/seimin), so instead of five raw knobs this is a single on/off that applies a vetted, known-good set (semin 2, semax 4, seup 3, sedn 2, seimin 1). It only acts above a velocity threshold, and being too aggressive under high acceleration can drop steps — leave it off unless you know you want it. Live only: a restart or “reset to config” restores your saved values.

registers.title: Editing registers (advanced)
registers.body: The advanced editor writes individual TMC tuning registers to the driver live — chopper timing (toff/tbl/hstrt/hend), StealthChop PWM (pwm_grad/pwm_ofs/…), CoolStep, StallGuard sensitivity, and the StealthChop↔SpreadCycle speed threshold (in mm/s). Which fields appear, and their valid range, come from the printer-side safety policy — not the browser — because the driver silently truncates an out-of-range value instead of erroring, so the server clamps and rejects. Raw current-scaling and short/overtemp-protection registers are deliberately not editable here (current goes through the recommender/run-current path; microsteps need a config change + restart). Every edit is live only: INIT_TMC (the “reset to config” button), a firmware restart, or a power-cycle restores your saved values. Riskier knobs need a per-field confirm, and all writes are refused while the printer is printing or paused.

recommend.title: Recommended tuning
recommend.body: Once a motor is assigned, FilaMind can compute a suggested run current and the StealthChop / SpreadCycle register values (pwm_grad, pwm_ofs, hstrt, hend) from the motor’s datasheet specs and your supply voltage — so it works even without the autotune add-on installed. The run current defaults to a conservative 70% of the motor’s rating. The result is diffed against your live config; you can then copy it to printer.cfg, or write it live behind a confirm (reversible with Revert, and refused while printing). If the autotune add-on is installed, you can run it instead.

tmcDriver.def: The chip that turns Klipper’s step/dir signals into the coil currents that spin a stepper motor — e.g. TMC2209, TMC2240, TMC5160. Each motor has one.

runCurrent.def: How hard the motor is driven while moving (amps). Too low → skipped steps; too high → overheating. The live value can differ slightly from the configured one because the chip quantises it.

holdCurrent.def: The reduced current applied while the motor is idle, to save heat and noise.

microsteps.def: How finely each full motor step is subdivided (16/32/…/256). Higher = smoother and quieter motion, with no real loss of torque thanks to interpolation.

stealthChop.def: A near-silent driving mode, great for Z and idle. Smooth but slightly less precise at high speed.

spreadCycle.def: A precise, high-torque driving mode, better for fast X/Y moves. Audible but accurate.

stallGuard.def: The driver’s load sensor. It enables sensorless homing (detecting a stall instead of using an endstop switch). Its threshold register varies by model (sgthrs / sgt / sg4_thrs).

senseResistor.def: A tiny resistor that sets the driver’s current scale. It must match your board; the dashboard shows the configured value.

interface.def: How the MCU talks to the driver. UART is a single wire (TMC220x family); SPI is a faster multi-wire bus used by the performance parts (2130 / 5160). The TMC2240 can do either.

stallguard.signed: Signed −64…63 — LOWER is more sensitive (stops sooner). Raise it if the axis stops short; lower it if it never stops.
stallguard.unsigned: Range 0–255 — HIGHER is more sensitive (stops sooner). Lower it if the axis stops short; raise it if it never stops.

widget.intro: Every TMC stepper driver on your printer, read live from its Klipper config — inspect, get tuning recommendations, and apply them (gated). New here? Try 🧭 Guided.
steps: Each card is one stepper driver — its heading is the axis (X / Y / Z / Extruder) and the badge is the chip model.
Run current shows how hard the motor is driven; the live value may differ slightly from the configured “set” value — that’s normal.
Mode tells you SpreadCycle (precise, louder) or StealthChop (quiet); microsteps shows how smooth the motion is.
Home an axis or jog the motor to wake it — live temperature and fault flags only appear while a motor is enabled (idle drivers show “idle”).
Open “advanced registers” on a card to inspect the raw tuning values the driver is using.
widget.unreachable: Can’t reach the printer’s Moonraker — driver data is unavailable right now.
widget.noDrivers: No TMC drivers found in the Klipper config. (Only TMC stepper drivers appear here.)
widget.healthTitleIdle: Enable the motor (home or jog an axis) to read live temperature and fault flags

liveMonitor.idle: Idle — enable the motor (home or jog the axis) to read live SG / current / temperature.

recommendPanel.basis: run current = {basis}; StealthChop holds to ~{rps} rev/s at {voltage} V.
recommendPanel.confirm: Write these to the {stepper} driver now. Reversible (Revert / a restart restores your config); refused while printing.
recommendPanel.autotuneTitle: Runs AUTOTUNE_TMC if the klipper_tmc_autotune add-on is installed
recommendPanel.noSpecs: No datasheet specs for this motor — tuning recommendation unavailable.

motorSync.about: Aligns the microstep phase of multiple motors on one axis (dual / quad-Z, dual-X) using an accelerometer, so they don't fight each other. Provided by the separate {addon} add-on.
motorSync.notInstalled: Not installed on this printer. Install the motors_sync add-on to enable it here.
motorSync.warning: ⚠ This moves the toolhead for a while (it probes each motor). Keep clear and ready to cut power.
motorSync.confirm: I'm watching the printer — run motor synchronization now.

sensorless.noRegister: ⚠ This driver exposes no StallGuard threshold register, so sensorless homing can’t be tuned here — check the printer’s homing configuration.
sensorless.confirmSet: Write this threshold to the driver now (reversible; refused while printing).
sensorless.testHomeWarning: ⚠ Test-home moves the {axis} axis now. With a wrong threshold it may not stop — keep a hand on the power. Sensorless axes only.
sensorless.confirmHome: I’m watching the printer and ready to cut power — home {axis}.

homing.physicalIntroLead: This axis homes against a physical endstop switch
homing.physicalIntroTail: . Press the switch by hand and re-check to confirm the wiring before homing.
homing.testHomeWarn: ⚠ Test-home moves the {axis} axis to its endstop now — make sure the path is clear.
homing.probeNote: This axis homes with the Z probe (the same sensor used for bed mesh), so there’s no StallGuard threshold to tune here. Set the probe offset and mesh in the bed-leveling tools.
homing.virtualNote: This axis homes against a virtual endstop that isn’t a TMC StallGuard pin — it’s managed by its own host module, so there’s nothing to tune here.

guidedWizard.title: 🧭 Guided tuning
guidedWizard.step2Intro: Which motor is wired to this axis? Pick it so FilaMind knows its datasheet specs.
guidedWizard.step3Intro: Get a recommendation from the motor's specs, then copy it to your config or apply it live (gated).
guidedWizard.step4Intro: Optional: dial in sensorless homing. Skip if this axis uses an endstop switch.
guidedWizard.step5Intro: Done with {axis}. Watch it live while you move the axis, or tune another.

registerEditor.resetTitle: INIT_TMC — re-apply this driver's configured registers + current
registerEditor.liveOnly: Edits are {live} — not saved. {initTmc} (reset), a firmware restart, or a power-cycle restores the configured values. Out-of-range values are rejected by the printer-side safety policy; raw current and protection registers aren't editable here.
registerEditor.coolstepEnableTitle: Apply the autotune-vetted CoolStep set (semin 2 / semax 4 / seup 3 / sedn 2 / seimin 1)
registerEditor.toffHint: If you set toff to 1, also keep tbl ≥ 1 (the 16-cycle blank time needs it).

apply.busyApply: Refusing to write to a driver while the printer is busy (printing or paused).
apply.busyReinit: Refusing to re-init while the printer is busy (printing or paused).
apply.reinitialized: Re-initialized {stepper} and restored its configured current.
apply.autotuneNotInstalled: klipper_tmc_autotune is not installed for this stepper — use the recommendation or copy-to-config instead.
apply.busyAutotune: Refusing to autotune while the printer is busy (printing or paused).
apply.busyWrite: Refusing to write while the printer is busy (printing or paused).
apply.fieldSet: Set {field} = {num} on {stepper} (live only — INIT_TMC or a restart restores the configured value).
apply.coolstepEnabled: CoolStep enabled on {stepper} (live only — INIT_TMC or a restart restores the configured values).
apply.coolstepDisabled: CoolStep disabled on {stepper} (live only — INIT_TMC or a restart restores the configured values).
apply.busyHome: Refusing to home while the printer is busy (printing or paused).
apply.motorsyncNotInstalled: The motors_sync add-on isn't installed — it aligns the microstep phase of multiple motors on one axis (dual/quad-Z, dual-X) using an accelerometer.
apply.busySync: Refusing to sync motors while the printer is busy (printing or paused).
apply.overCurrentCap: Refused: {requested} A on {stepper} exceeds the {cap} A ceiling (driver full-scale limit / rated current of the assigned motor).
